sanskrit: ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः । सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥
hindiTranslation: उसके बाद शंख, भेरी नगाड़े, ढोल, मृदङ्ग और नरसिंघे बाजे एक साथ बज उठे ।  उनका वह शब्द बड़ा भयंकर हुआ ॥
englishTranslation: Then conches, kettledrums, drums, and trumpets burst forth at once. Their sound became thunderous.
essence: The war announces itself before the first blow.
speaker: Sanjaya
simpleMeaning: Conches, drums, and trumpets all sounded together, and the noise became overwhelming.
simpleInsight: The battlefield is no longer quiet. The clash of war has begun to announce itself.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, after Bhishma's conch, the Kaurava side answers with a sudden roar of instruments. The battlefield fills with noise before the first arrows fly, building the tension that leads into Krishna and Arjuna sounding their own conches.
modernRelevance: A room goes silent before a hard meeting starts, then every phone buzzes at once. The moment of pressure arrives before a single word is spoken.
translationLiteral: ततः (then) / शङ्खाः (conches) / च (and) / भेर्यः (kettledrums) / च (and) / पणवानकगोमुखाः (small drums and trumpets) / सहसा एव (all at once) / अभ्यहन्यन्त (were struck) / सः (that) / शब्दः (sound) / तुमुलः (tumultuous) / अभवत् (became)
insight: It turns the scene from preparation into full confrontation.
